name: Claude Opus 4.8
description: Anthropic's flagship Opus model with state-of-the-art long-horizon agentic execution, knowledge work, and memory. 84% on Online-Mind2Web, dynamic multi-subagent workflows, ~4x less likely to miss its own code flaws than its predecessor, and 1M context at standard pricing.

evidence.value: State-of-the-art long-horizon agentic coding; ~4x less likely to miss flaws in its own code vs Opus 4.7
84% on Online-Mind2Web live web-agent benchmark

task_accuracy_code.methodology: Agentic coding and web-agent benchmarks measuring long-horizon autonomous execution and self-verification

evidence.value: State-of-the-art on knowledge work and memory tasks; improved planning via deeper per-step reasoning

task_accuracy_reasoning.methodology: Graduate-level reasoning and knowledge-work benchmarks evaluated with adaptive thinking at high effort

evidence.value: Gains across knowledge work, document tasks, and memory benchmarks over Opus 4.7

task_accuracy_general.methodology: Comprehensive knowledge and multimodal testing, including high-resolution vision inherited from Opus 4.7

evidence.value: ~4x reduction in missed self-authored code flaws; stronger self-verification across long agentic runs

output_consistency.methodology: Long-horizon agentic run consistency and self-verification testing across effort levels
output_consistency.notes: Same adaptive-thinking-only surface as Opus 4.7; effort parameter (incl. xhigh) controls depth

evidence.value: Typical response time ~2.8s for standard prompts at default effort; fast mode available at $10/$50

latency_p50.methodology: Median latency for API requests with standard prompt sizes

evidence.value: p95 ~6.5s; higher at xhigh/max effort

latency_p95.methodology: 95th percentile response time across diverse workloads

evidence.value: 1M token context at standard API pricing (no long-context premium), 128K max output
99.9%+ uptime (last 90 days)

uptime.methodology: Historical uptime data from official status page

performance_reliability.notes: Current flagship Opus. State-of-the-art long-horizon agentic execution, knowledge work, and memory; 84% Online-Mind2Web; dynamic multi-subagent workflows. Superseded only by the higher-tier Claude Fable 5.

evidence.value: Improved resistance to injected instructions in agentic and browsing contexts; mid-session system prompts (beta) provide an injection-safe operator channel

prompt_injection_resistance.methodology: Testing against OWASP LLM01 prompt injection attacks, including agentic browsing scenarios

evidence.value: Constitutional AI alignment with refined refusal calibration
Training opt-out by default for API; no training on user data without consent

data_leakage_prevention.methodology: Analysis of privacy policies and data handling practices

evidence.value: Released under the Responsible Scaling Policy with real-time cybersecurity safeguards carried forward from Opus 4.7

output_safety.methodology: Comprehensive safety testing across harmful content categories

evidence.value: API key and OAuth authentication, HTTPS only, rate limiting, workspace scoping

api_security.methodology: Review of API security features and best practices

security.notes: Strong safety posture with agentic-specific safeguards. Mid-session system prompts (beta) give operators a non-spoofable instruction channel for long-running sessions.

evidence.value: Data residency options for US and EU enterprise customers

data_residency.methodology: Review of enterprise documentation and privacy policies

evidence.value: Training opt-out by default for API usage

training_data_optout.methodology: Analysis of privacy policy and data usage terms

evidence.value: Zero data retention agreements available for eligible API customers

data_retention.methodology: Review of terms of service and data retention policies

evidence.value: Customer responsible for PII redaction; provider-side safeguards for incidental PII

pii_handling.methodology: Review of data protection capabilities and customer responsibilities

evidence.value: SOC 2 Type II, GDPR compliant, HIPAA eligible

compliance_certifications.methodology: Verification of compliance certifications and audit reports

evidence.value: Zero data retention configuration available; no training on API data by default

zero_data_retention.methodology: Review of data handling practices and trust center documentation

privacy_compliance.notes: Standard Anthropic enterprise compliance posture: SOC 2 Type II, GDPR, HIPAA-eligible, training opt-out by default for API traffic.

evidence.value: Adaptive thinking with effort control; richer user-facing narration during long agentic runs; thinking text omitted by default with summarized display opt-in

explainability.methodology: Evaluation of reasoning transparency and explanation capabilities

evidence.value: ~4x reduction in missed self-authored code flaws; improved factual grounding in knowledge work

hallucination_rate.methodology: Testing on factual QA datasets and self-verification evaluations

evidence.value: Regular bias testing and mitigation under the Responsible Scaling Policy

bias_fairness.methodology: Evaluation on bias benchmarks and diverse demographic testing

evidence.value: More deliberate: pauses to ask on ambiguous decisions and flags uncertainty rather than guessing

uncertainty_quantification.methodology: Qualitative assessment of confidence expression in outputs

evidence.value: Comprehensive model card with capabilities, limitations, benchmarks, and migration guidance

model_card_quality.methodology: Review of documentation completeness and clarity

evidence.value: General description provided, detailed sources not disclosed

training_data_transparency.methodology: Review of public disclosures about training data

evidence.value: Constitutional AI safety guardrails with real-time cybersecurity safeguards

trust_transparency.notes: More deliberate and transparent in long agentic runs than Opus 4.7 — narrates progress, flags uncertainty, and self-verifies code. Thinking text remains omitted by default.

evidence.value: Same API surface as Opus 4.7 — no new breaking changes; adds mid-session system prompts (beta)

api_design_quality.methodology: Review of API design, consistency, and feature completeness
api_design_quality.notes: Adaptive thinking only; effort levels include xhigh; task budgets (beta) supported

evidence.value: Official SDKs (Python, TypeScript, Java, Go, Ruby, C#, PHP) with day-one support

sdk_quality.methodology: Review of SDK quality, documentation, and maintenance

evidence.value: Clear versioning with advance deprecation notice; stable claude-opus-4-8 alias

versioning_policy.methodology: Review of versioning policy and historical practices

evidence.value: Usage dashboard with metrics, cost tracking, and workspace controls

monitoring_observability.methodology: Review of available monitoring tools and metrics

evidence.value: Email support, developer community, comprehensive docs and a dedicated 4.7-to-4.8 migration guide

support_quality.methodology: Assessment of documentation, community, and support responsiveness

evidence.value: Available on the Anthropic API and major cloud platforms; default model in Claude Code and the Agent SDK

ecosystem_maturity.methodology: Analysis of third-party integrations and availability surfaces

evidence.value: Standard commercial terms; enterprise agreements available

license_terms.methodology: Review of licensing terms and restrictions

operational_excellence.notes: Drop-in upgrade from Opus 4.7 (identical API surface). 1M context at standard pricing with no long-context premium; optional fast mode at $10/$50.

code-generation.notes: State-of-the-art long-horizon agentic coding; ~4x less likely to miss its own code flaws than Opus 4.7. Best value flagship for software engineering at $5/$25.

customer-support.notes: Excellent quality with warmer, clearer writing than Opus 4.7, but Sonnet/Haiku tiers are more cost-effective for routine volume.

content-creation.notes: Clearer, warmer, less hedged prose than prior Opus models — approaches expert-level structure at higher effort.

data-analysis.notes: Strong analytical depth with 1M context for whole-dataset work; dynamic multi-subagent workflows fan out across large analyses.

research-assistant.notes: State-of-the-art knowledge work and memory; excels at multi-day research with file-based memory and 1M context.

legal-compliance.notes: Strong privacy posture (SOC 2 Type II, GDPR, HIPAA-eligible) and thorough long-document analysis at 1M context with no long-context premium.

healthcare.notes: HIPAA eligible with training opt-out by default. Deliberate, uncertainty-flagging behavior suits clinical documentation.

financial-analysis.notes: Excellent quantitative reasoning and knowledge work; handles full filings and model workbooks in one context window.

education.notes: Clear, warm explanations with effort-adjustable depth; strong thought-partner behavior that pushes back constructively.

creative-writing.notes: Warmer, less hedged voice with fewer AI vocal tics than 4.7. No sampling parameters — variety must be prompted.

strengths: State-of-the-art long-horizon agentic execution, knowledge work, and memory
~4x less likely to miss flaws in its own code than Opus 4.7
Dynamic multi-subagent workflows for parallel fan-out
1M context at standard pricing (no long-context premium), 128K output
Mid-session system prompts (beta) — injection-safe operator channel that preserves prompt cache
Same API surface as Opus 4.7 — drop-in upgrade with no new breaking changes
limitations: Adaptive thinking only — no manual thinking budgets, no temperature/top_p sampling parameters
More deliberate by default: asks clarifying questions more often unless granted explicit autonomy
Narrates more between tool calls than 4.7 — needs a silence-default prompt for terse agents
Conservative about reaching for search, subagents, and custom tools without explicit triggering guidance
Higher latency than Sonnet/Haiku tiers; fast mode doubles cost to $10/$50
best_for: Long-horizon autonomous coding runs and complex refactors
Knowledge work over very large document sets (1M context, no premium)
Memory-dependent agents that persist context across sessions
not_recommended_for: Cost-sensitive high-volume inference (use Sonnet or Haiku tiers)
Workflows that depend on temperature/top_p sampling controls

pricing.notes: Fast mode available at $10/$50 per 1M. 1M context at standard pricing with no long-context premium. Batch API 50% discount, prompt caching savings apply.

metadata.architecture: Transformer-based with Constitutional AI alignment; adaptive thinking only with effort parameter including xhigh; same API surface as Opus 4.7
